nodes.title: Return early for empty input
nodes.why: It avoids extra work and keeps edge-case handling obvious.
nodes.alternative: Let the chain run and rely on split/map/join to produce an empty result.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more code, but clearer intent for readers.

nodes.title: Split by spaces before transforming
nodes.why: This turns the sentence into word-like chunks so each part can be normalized.
nodes.alternative: Use regex with word boundaries and replace directly.
nodes.tradeoff: Easy to follow, though punctuation rules are less explicit than regex-based parsing.

nodes.title: Uppercase first character, lowercase the rest
nodes.why: This guarantees a consistent title-case style for each token.
nodes.alternative: Only uppercase the first letter and keep remaining letters unchanged.
nodes.tradeoff: Consistent output, but it intentionally rewrites existing mixed-case words.

nodes.title: Track whether the next letter starts a word
nodes.why: A simple boolean state keeps the traversal logic predictable.
nodes.alternative: Check the previous character on every iteration.
nodes.tradeoff: State is easy to reason about, but you must keep it in sync carefully.

nodes.title: Reset state when a space is seen
nodes.why: Spaces separate words, so the following non-space character should be capitalized.
nodes.alternative: Split into words first instead of handling separators manually.
nodes.tradeoff: Gives precise control over spacing behavior, with more branching logic.

nodes.title: Use explicit uppercase/lowercase branches
nodes.why: The branch makes it clear when to capitalize and when to normalize to lowercase.
nodes.alternative: Build whole words first and transform after each word ends.
nodes.tradeoff: Very explicit control flow, but more verbose than a split-map-join approach.
